hero.question: JD Vance diplomatic meeting with Iran by April 10?
hero.prob_take: A 20% probability for JD Vance meeting Iranian officials by April 10 prices in almost zero chance of a diplomatic thaw before the deadline, but that ignores the speed at which geopolitical surprises can move a new administration. The 10-point drop in 24 hours suggests panic selling rather than hard news, and with 6 weeks left on the clock, bettors are overweighting the status quo and underweighting backchannels that operate faster than public statements signal.

daily_take.headline: Vance-Iran Talk Odds Crater 10 Points. Market Pricing in Cold War, Not Diplomacy.
daily_take.deck: JD Vance meeting Iran by April 10 dropped from 30% to 20% in 24 hours on zero new headlines. The market is treating this as pure fantasy, but a 10-point swing this sharp usually means someone knows something. Either the Trump admin just signaled "not happening" to insiders, or the market is dramatically underweighting a backdoor channel that already exists. Smart money is asking: why would odds move this hard without a news trigger? That's usually when the real trade lives.

sidebar.headline: Vance-Iran bilateral talks collapsed into the same 21% pricing as the broader US-Iran meeting odds. Convergence this tight suggests the market sees no daylight between a solo Vance trip and full diplomatic engagement.
sidebar.label: US-Iran Meeting: 21.1%

sidebar.headline: KKR-LSG props down 25 points while tournament volume stays healthy. Someone is hedging big holdings or IPL season sentiment just shifted hard.
sidebar.label: KKR vs LSG: 22.5%

sidebar.headline: Hormuz traffic odds barely budged despite -11 point drop in both Iran meetings. Market still pricing 21.5% chance of normal shipping by end of April despite diplomatic freeze narrative tightening.
sidebar.label: Hormuz Normal Traffic: 21.5%
